question: A 45-year-old woman with palpitations. ECG: irregularly irregular, absent P waves, HR 130.

explanation.why_correct: The presentation is classic for Atrial Fibrillation. This is a ecg_interpretation case requiring prompt recognition.

why_wrong.Atrial Flutter: Atrial Flutter has different presentation and findings than Atrial Fibrillation.
why_wrong.SVT: SVT has different presentation and findings than Atrial Fibrillation.
why_wrong.Sinus Tachycardia: Sinus Tachycardia has different presentation and findings than Atrial Fibrillation.

media.description: 12-Lead ECG - Interpret the rhythm and findings

question: A 25-year-old man with progressive dyspnea, orthopnea, edema. Echo: LVEF 30%.

explanation.why_correct: The presentation is classic for Systolic Heart Failure. This is a diagnosis case requiring prompt recognition.

why_wrong.Diastolic HF: Diastolic HF has different presentation and findings than Systolic Heart Failure.
why_wrong.COPD: COPD has different presentation and findings than Systolic Heart Failure.
why_wrong.PE: PE has different presentation and findings than Systolic Heart Failure.

media.description: Clinical image - Use to guide your diagnosis

question: A 45-year-old man with acute chest pain, BP 85/60, JVD, muffled heart sounds, electrical alternans.

explanation.why_correct: The presentation is classic for Cardiac Tamponade. This is a emergency_reasoning case requiring prompt recognition.

why_wrong.Tension PTX: Tension PTX has different presentation and findings than Cardiac Tamponade.
why_wrong.Massive PE: Massive PE has different presentation and findings than Cardiac Tamponade.
why_wrong.Cardiogenic Shock: Cardiogenic Shock has different presentation and findings than Cardiac Tamponade.

media.description: Emergency ECG - Identify the life-threatening rhythm

question: A 72-year-old woman with crushing chest pain radiating to left arm. ECG: ST elevation V1-V4.

explanation.why_correct: The presentation is classic for Anterior STEMI. This is a ecg_interpretation case requiring prompt recognition.

why_wrong.NSTEMI: NSTEMI has different presentation and findings than Anterior STEMI.
why_wrong.Pericarditis: Pericarditis has different presentation and findings than Anterior STEMI.
why_wrong.Aortic Dissection: Aortic Dissection has different presentation and findings than Anterior STEMI.
